sections.paragraph:
τυρβάζω (τύρβη ‘disorder, confusion’; Ps.-Hes, Soph. et al.) to cause to be stirred up mid. or pass. in act. sense trouble oneself, be troubled, be agitated περί τι with or about someth. (Aristoph., Pax 1007) περὶ πολλά Lk 10:41 v.l. (Nilus, Ep. 2, 258 μὴ ἄγαν τυρβάζου).—DELG s.v. τύρβη.

Ps. Ps. = pseudo, pseudonymous

Hes Hes , date uncertain, perh. before VI b.c.—List 5

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

act. act. = active

someth. someth. = something

Aristoph Aristoph , V–IV b.c.—List 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Ep.

Ep. = Epistola/Epistula, when applied to letters mostly pseudonymous; various dates—List 5

  1. = epistle

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1021
